codeReview.summary: The feature correctly makes marketplace rows read-only and switches the markdown/path/install UI by source, but duplicate Claude rows still share AI explanation state and cache by logical skill id. Switching between marketplace and user copies can therefore surface the wrong explanation even though the rest of the detail view updates.

issues.description: After a duplicate Claude row switch, the view reloads cached explanations with `detailRequest.skillId` only and generate/refresh also use `skillId` only (`src/components/skill/SkillDetailView.tsx:345-346,410-421`). The store and backend use the same logical-skill key (`src/stores/skillDetailStore.ts:224-285`; `src-tauri/src/commands/marketplace.rs:843-896,1300-1344`), so marketplace and user copies of the same skill share one explanation cache. If an explanation is generated or cached for one source row, opening the other row can still show that prior source's explanation instead of preserving source-specific detail state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend worker skill's duplicate-row verification guidance only spells out metadata/content/action switching, and the example handoff mirrors that scope. It never explicitly calls out the AI Explanation tab even though that is part of the detail surface, which likely helped this row-specific explanation leak survive validation.

The shared source-aware detail API note documents row-aware detail loading only through `get_skill_detail(...rowId)` plus `read_file_by_path(detail.file_path)`. It does not mention that any detail-surface state derived from cached AI explanations also needs row-aware identity when 'source-specific detail state' is required, so future workers could miss the same edge again.

The worker's handoff reports a clean scoped commit but also notes that unrelated pre-existing dirty files made global `git status` noisy. The shared worker-base success checklist appears not to account for mission-scoped commits in an already-dirty repo, which forces workers to explain the mismatch manually.

summary: Fail: the read-only/source-path UI work landed, but duplicate Claude rows still share AI explanation state keyed only by `skillId`, so the detail surface is not fully source-specific.
